questions.text: Which missingness mechanism occurs when the probability of missing data depends on unobserved parameters or the missing value itself?
questions.explanation: MNAR occurs when the missingness depends on the value of the missing variable itself, which is unobserved (e.g., individuals with high incomes refusing to disclose their wealth).

questions.text: Why does Mahalanobis distance outperform Euclidean distance for multi-dimensional anomaly detection?
options: Mahalanobis distance is computationally faster as it avoids matrix transpositions.
Mahalanobis distance accounts for variance scales and linear correlations between features by incorporating the inverse covariance matrix.
Euclidean distance cannot be calculated in spaces with dimensions greater than three.
Mahalanobis distance automatically imputes missing variables during distance calculations.
questions.correct_answer: Mahalanobis distance accounts for variance scales and linear correlations between features by incorporating the inverse covariance matrix.
questions.explanation: By scaling and rotating coordinate systems using the inverse covariance matrix, Mahalanobis distance adjusts for variations in scale and dependencies between variables, creating a normalized distance metric for correlated features.
